text: When a program doesn't work as expected, the first step is identifying bugs by carefully observing what happens versus what should happen. A systematic approach helps: reproduce the issue, check recent changes, and isolate the problematic section of code. Debugging tools like breakpoints allow you to pause execution and inspect variable values step by step. Logging is also valuable—adding print statements or using a logging library to track what's happening at key points. By following a logical method and using these tools, you can find and fix errors more efficiently without guessing randomly.
